zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Woodthorpe於大約1255年出生在英國林可爾史爾薩利比 煙雨 雷斯索普時，她的父親WILLIAM DE WOODTHORPE II36歲，母親Alice of Lancashire43歲。她於大約1278年與Hugh Bernanke在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾薩利比結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世在英國越可爾多卡斯特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Woodthorpe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name mainly from Woodthorpe in Strubby (Lincs), though occasionally from Woodthorpe in Loughborough (Leics). There is no evidence to suggest that the surname could be from other like-named places, such as Woodthorpe, Woodthorpe in Staveley (Derbys), Woodthorpe Hall in Handsworth, or Woodthorpe in Sandal Magna (WR Yorks), except perhaps for the early bearer from 1251–2.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Woodthorpe was born about 1255, in Saleby with Thoresthorpe, Lincolnshire, England, her father, WILLIAM DE WOODTHORPE II, was 36 and her mother, Alice of Lancashire, was 43. She married Hugh Bernanke about 1278, in Saleby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died in Doncaster, Yorkshire, England.

Elle a épousé Hugh Bernanke environ 1278, à Saleby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée à Doncaster, Everwyck, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud de Woodthorpeが約1255年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアサレビー ウィス ソアスソルペで生まれた時，父WILLIAM DE WOODTHORPE IIは36歳，母Alice of Lancashireは43歳であった。約1278年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアサレビーでHugh Bernankeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女はイギリスヨークシュアイアドンカスターで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Woodthorpe(이)가 대략 1255년 영국 링코실르 살일비 위스 토레스소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 WILLIAM DE WOODTHORPE II(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice of Lancashire(은)는 43세였다. 그녀는 대략 1278년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 살일비에서 Hugh Bernanke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 요르크실르 돈카스터에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud de Woodthorpe родилась около 1255 г. в местности сеилеби вит таурестаурп, линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени WILLIAM DE WOODTHORPE II было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice of Lancashire, 43. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Hugh Bernanke около 1278 г. в местности сеилеби, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла в местности донкастир, еркшир, Англия.
